text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English. I'm Neil. And I'm Bekka.
zh: 这里是BBC英语学习的《六分钟英语》。我是Neil。我是Bekka。

text: In this episode, we're discussing a food seasoning that's so popular we eat around three -quarters of a million tonnes of it a year.
zh: 在这一期节目中,我们要聊一种非常受欢迎的食物调料,我们每年要吃掉大约75万吨。

text: We're talking about pepper. Do you add pepper to your food, Bekka?
zh: 我们说的是胡椒。Bekka,你会在食物里加胡椒吗?

text: I do, Neil. Yes, I think it's an easy way to add some spice.
zh: 会的,Neil。我觉得这是增添一点辣味的简单方法。

text: Yeah, I love a bit of pepper. I grind pepper onto everything. Well, not everything, but I do like it.
zh: 是啊,我很喜欢胡椒。我几乎在所有东西上都会磨点胡椒。好吧,也不是所有东西,但我确实喜欢它。

text: Given its popularity, it's surprising that most people know very little about pepper.
zh: 考虑到它这么受欢迎,让人惊讶的是,大多数人对胡椒其实知之甚少。

text: Did you know, for example, that peppers are the fruit of vines, often growing over 10 metres high?
zh: 比如说,你知道胡椒其实是藤本植物的果实吗?这些藤蔓常常能长到十多米高。

text: Our ancestors would be surprised how little we know. From ancient Greece onwards, downwards,
zh: 我们的祖先要是知道我们懂得这么少,一定会很惊讶。从古希腊开始,一路传承下来,

text: pepper was prized as the black gold of ingredients, and explorers crossed oceans in search of it, discovering new continents along the way.
zh: 胡椒曾被视为“食材界的黑金”,探险家们为了寻找它跨越重洋,并在途中发现了新大陆。

text: In this episode, we'll get reacquainted with pepper and learn some useful new words and phrases too.
zh: 在这一期节目中,我们将重新认识胡椒,并学习一些实用的新词汇和短语。

text: And remember, you'll find a transcript for you to read along with us as you listen on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 别忘了,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到文字稿,边听边看。

text: OK, first I have a question for you of course, Bekka. Although it's black pepper,
zh: 好了,Bekka,我照例先来问你一个问题。虽然在英国的商店和餐厅里,

text: you're most likely to see in shops and restaurants in the UK, there are hundreds of different varieties worldwide.
zh: 你最常见到的是黑胡椒,但全世界其实有数百个不同的品种。

text: But what is unusual about phu quoc, a white pepper from Vietnam?
zh: 那么,越南的富国白胡椒有什么与众不同之处呢?

text: Does it a make people cry, b taste like parmesan cheese, or c cost more than gold?
zh: 是A让人流泪,B味道像帕玛森芝士,还是C比黄金还贵?

text: Hmm, well I don't think it would be as expensive as gold, and I kind of want it to taste like parmesan cheese.
zh: 嗯,我觉得它应该不会比黄金还贵,而且我挺希望它味道像帕玛森芝士的。

text: OK, well, we'll find out later in the programme.
zh: 好,我们稍后揭晓答案。

text: Mathilde Rollinger is the daughter of Olivier Rollinger, an award -winning French chef famous for his use of spices.
zh: Mathilde Rollinger是法国名厨Olivier Rollinger的女儿,她父亲因擅长运用香料而屡获殊荣。

text: While other little girls of her age were sprinkling sugar on their breakfast yoghurt,
zh: 当同龄的小女孩们在早餐酸奶里撒糖的时候,

text: Mathilde was the only girl in Paris sprinkling pepper.
zh: Mathilde却是巴黎唯一一个往酸奶里撒胡椒的女孩。

text: Today, Mathilde runs the Épice Rollinger spice shop in the Opera area of the city.
zh: 如今,Mathilde在巴黎歌剧院区经营着Épices Rollinger香料店。

text: Here, customers can can find a huge range of peppers,
zh: 在这里,顾客可以找到种类繁多的胡椒,

text: from fruity red Cambodian Kampot pepper to Borneo's Sarawak black pepper with its woody aroma.
zh: 从果香浓郁的柬埔寨贡布红胡椒,到带有木质香气的婆罗洲砂拉越黑胡椒,应有尽有。

text: These peppers have strong, distinctive tastes.
zh: 这些胡椒味道浓烈、各具特色。

text: But curiously, they enhance rather than overpower the flavour of the food you're eating,
zh: 但奇妙的是,它们并不会压过食物本身的味道,反而能提升风味,

text: as Mathilde explained to BBC World Service programme The Food Chain.
zh: 正如Mathilde在BBC世界服务频道节目《The Food Chain》中所解释的那样。

text: It will give a kick and transform it, but you will still have the savour of the different
zh: 它会带来一种冲击感,改变食物的风味,但你依然能尝到各种食材本身的味道。

text: ingredients. It will not disguise the other ingredients, but it will push them.
zh: 它不会掩盖其他食材的味道,而是会把它们的味道衬托出来。

text: It's a flavour catalyst. We can say in punctuation, like it's an exclamation.
zh: 它是一种“风味催化剂”。用标点符号来打比方的话,它就像一个感叹号。

text: It's like an exclamation mark. Exactly. Mathilde says pepper gives food a kick.
zh: 就像感叹号一样。没错。Mathilde说胡椒能给食物带来一种“刺激感”。

text: To give something a kick means to provide it with extra stimulation or excitement. Pepper also makes flavours more intense.
zh: “give something a kick”意思是给某样东西增添额外的刺激或兴奋感。胡椒还能让食物的味道更浓烈。

text: Mathilde calls it a catalyst – something that causes another action to start or makes it happen more quickly.
zh: Mathilde称它为“催化剂”——指能促使另一件事发生,或让它更快发生的东西。

text: In fact, she says pepper is like an exclamation mark.
zh: 事实上,她说胡椒就像一个感叹号。

text: Saying something is like an exclamation mark means it shows strong emotion or excitement –
zh: 说某样东西“像感叹号一样”,意思是它表现出强烈的情绪或兴奋感——

text: the same thing an exclamation mark does in written punctuation.
zh: 就像书面标点中感叹号所起的作用一样。

text: Mathilde's spice shop holds pepper tasting sessions where she explains to customers the origin of her peppers
zh: Mathilde的香料店会举办胡椒品鉴会,向顾客讲解各种胡椒的产地,

text: and how they grow, changing colour as they harden in the sun.
zh: 以及它们的生长过程——在阳光下逐渐变硬、颜色也随之变化。

text: Reporter John Lawrenson attended one of these tasting sessions for BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: 记者John Lawrenson为BBC世界服务频道节目《The Food Chain》参加了其中一场品鉴会。

text: The different colours of pepper though, as Mathilde started to say,
zh: 不过,正如Mathilde一开始所说,胡椒的不同颜色,

text: do not correspond to the different varieties but to the maturity of the peppercorns and what people do to them.
zh: 并不对应不同的品种,而是取决于胡椒粒的成熟程度,以及人们对它做了怎样的处理。

text: They're green when they're young, black when they're mature and dried, red when they're very mature.
zh: 它们年轻时是绿色的,成熟晒干后是黑色的,而完全成熟时则是红色的。

text: Grey pepper is an industrial creation, not a botanical one, ground to a fine powder.
zh: 灰胡椒是一种人工加工品,而非天然品种,是把胡椒磨成细粉制成的。

text: It is, says Mathilde, grey dust. She's not very keen on that one.
zh: Mathilde说,那其实就是“灰色的粉末”。她不太喜欢这种胡椒。

text: The colour of pepper is not determined by the variety, but by its maturity.
zh: 胡椒的颜色并不是由品种决定的,而是由其成熟程度决定的。

text: A food's maturity describes the stage when a food is fully grown and ready to harvest.
zh: 食物的“成熟度”指的是食物完全长成、可以采收的阶段。

text: For peppers, this is when they wrinkle and go black.
zh: 对胡椒来说,这个阶段就是果实起皱、变黑的时候。

text: Often a pepper mill is used to grind pepper, to crush it into powder by pressing it between two hard surfaces.
zh: 人们常用胡椒研磨器来磨胡椒,通过把它夹在两个坚硬的表面之间挤压,把它碾成粉末。

text: This happens with grey pepper, an artificially produced pepper mix which Mathilde is not keen on, meaning she doesn't like it.
zh: 灰胡椒就是这样制成的,它是一种人工调配的胡椒粉,而Mathilde对此并不“keen on”,也就是说她并不喜欢它。

text: We've learned so much about pepper, I'll look at it differently the next time I sprinkle some on my food.
zh: 我们学到了这么多关于胡椒的知识,下次我往食物上撒胡椒时,一定会用不同的眼光看待它了。

text: OK Neil, I think it's time to reveal the answer to your question.
zh: 好了,Neil,我想是时候揭晓你那个问题的答案了。

text: Yes, I asked you what's unusual about phu quoc, a white pepper from Vietnam.
zh: 是的,我问的是,越南的富国白胡椒有什么与众不同之处。

text: I answered B because I want it to taste like parmesan cheese.
zh: 我选了B,因为我希望它味道像帕玛森芝士。

text: Well, you're lucky because it is in fact B. It tastes like parmesan cheese. Well done.
zh: 你很幸运,答案正是B。它的味道确实像帕玛森芝士。答对了。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with the phrase give something a kick, meaning to add extra thrill or excitement.
zh: 好了,我们来复习一下今天学到的词汇,首先是短语“give something a kick”,意思是增添额外的刺激或兴奋感。

text: A catalyst causes something to start or speeds it up.
zh: “Catalyst”(催化剂)指促使某事发生,或加快其发生速度的东西。

text: If you say something is like an exclamation mark,
zh: 如果你说某样东西“像感叹号一样”,

text: you mean it signifies strong emotion, surprise or excitement, just like an exclamation mark does in writing.
zh: 意思是它体现出强烈的情绪、惊讶或兴奋,就像书面语中的感叹号一样。

text: A food's maturity refers to the stage when it's fully grown and ready for harvest.
zh: 食物的“maturity”(成熟度)指的是它完全长成、可以采收的阶段。

text: To grind food means to crush it into powder by being pressed between two hard surfaces.
zh: “Grind”(研磨)指通过把食物夹在两个坚硬表面之间挤压,把它碾成粉末。

text: And finally, if you're keen on something, you like it and enjoy doing it.
zh: 最后,如果你“keen on”某样东西,就是说你喜欢它,并乐在其中。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up. But remember,
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了。不过别忘了,

text: you can find worksheets, quizzes and loads more resources to improve your English on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到练习题、小测验以及更多提升英语水平的资源。

text: See you there soon, but for now it's Goodbye.
zh: 我们很快再见,现在先说再见啦。
